name: TRANSIENT REGIONAL OSTEOPOROSIS
definition: TRANSIENT BONE MARROW EDEMA rapid onset of self-limiting episodes of severe localized osteoporosis and pain but variant of regional migratory osteoporosis with more focal pattern of osteoporosis, which self-limiting disease of unknown etiology

demographics.Location: usually lower extremity (ie, ankle, knee, hip, foot)

dominant_finding: affected area has low SI on T1WI, high SI on T2WI (= bone marrow edema)
distribution: usually lower extremity (ie, ankle, knee, hip, foot)
discriminating_features: virtually PATHOGNOMONIC striking loss of subchondral cortex of femoral head + neck
rapid localized osteoporosis within 4-8 weeks after onset migrating from one joint to
no joint space narrowing / bone erosion

general: rapid localized osteoporosis within 4-8 weeks after onset migrating from one joint to
no joint space narrowing / bone erosion
MR: affected area has low SI on T1WI, high SI on T2WI (= bone marrow edema)
diffuse bone marrow edema involving femoral head + neck + sometimes intertrochanteric
NUC: progressive marked osteoporosis of femoral head, neck, acetabulum (3-8 weeks after onset
virtually PATHOGNOMONIC striking loss of subchondral cortex of femoral head + neck
NO joint space narrowing / subchondral bone collapse
markedly increased uptake on bone scan without cold spots / inhomogeneities (positive

clinical.Prognosis: persists for 6-9 months in one area; cycle of symptoms may last for several years
